In a ~400°F Convection Oven, roast the 1,2,4,5, and 6 ingredients until dark brown but not burnt
Transfer to tilt skillet omitting fat, and cover with Ingredient 3 (Chicken Stock), heat at 425°F, and bring to a boil
Reduce heat to 325°F and reduce volume by half
Once reduced, strain stock from tilt skillet and squeeze bones and veg for all liquid.  Should result in 22qt yield
Reserve Browned Stock overnight, skim excess fat on top next day before returning to tilt skillet.
Make a sachet of the herbs (bay leaf, thyme, parsley) and bring the Browned Stock to a boil then reduce heat and simmer
Reduce Browned stock to 10qt, then remove sachet.
Once reduced, add chicken fat, Mother #2, and Chicken Powder.  Ensure ingredients are thoroughly mixed.
Mix water and corn starch into a slurry, then slowly add to the fortified and reduced Browned Stock, mixing while pouring
Once the starch has activated and is thoroughly mixed in, ~10 minutes boil, strain through a chinois x3 times.
Reserve in a 12 quart cambro and cool in the blast chiller or ice bath.
Reserve in a quart containers and store in the cooler or freezer.
